records.summary: A four-panel coronary angiogram display from a cath-lab monitor showing sequential images of the coronary arteries during a percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI). Annotations indicate 99% stenosis and stent placement. The study date is 04-Apr-2026.
key_observations: Four fluoroscopic images from a coronary angiogram procedure.
Image 6 shows 99% stenosis in the left coronary system.
Image 27 shows a stent deployed in the vessel.
Multiple projections (RAO, LAO, CRAN, CAUD) are used to visualize the coronary tree.
Study date is 04-Apr-2026.

limitations: Image is a photograph of a monitor screen, not a direct medical image.
UI elements from the printing software are visible on the left side.

records.stage_evidence: Successful PTCA to LAD done (1-DES), TIMI-III flow achieved

records.summary: This is a typed PTCA (Percutaneous Transluminal Coronary Angioplasty) report for a 43-year-old male patient. The procedure was performed via transradial access on the left anterior descending (LAD) artery to treat a 99% stenosis. A Premier 3.0x36mm stent was deployed successfully, achieving TIMI-III flow. The report includes patient demographics, procedure details, and a recommendation for regular medicines and follow-up.
key_observations: Patient is a 43-year-old male.
Procedure: PTCA to LAD artery via transradial route.
Lesion: 99% stenosis in proximal part, 18mm length.
Stent deployed: Premier 3.0x36mm @ 14 ATM.
Post-dilation: Apollo 3.0x08mm @ 18 ATM.
Outcome: Successful PTCA, TIMI-III flow achieved.
Diagnosis: AWM I, CAD-SVD, Primary PCI to LAD (1-DES).
Recommendation: Regular medicines and follow-up.

records.stage_evidence: Report includes diagnosis and recommendation for PCI

records.summary: This is a typed coronary angiography report for a 43-year-old male patient. The report details findings of a 99% lesion in the proximal left anterior descending artery (LAD) and plaque in the distal right coronary artery (RCA). The diagnosis is Acute Wall Motion Abnormality (AWMI) and Coronary Artery Disease - Single Vessel Predominant (CAD-SVP). The recommendation is Primary Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) to the LAD using a 1-DES stent.
key_observations: Patient is a 43-year-old male with clinical profile AWM I
99% lesion in proximal LAD (18mm length) with normal mid and distal parts
Plaque noted in distal RCA, proximal and mid parts are normal
Diagnosis: AWM I, CAD-SVP
Recommendation: Primary PCI to LAD with 1-DES stent

records.summary: A four-frame coronary angiogram from a cath-lab monitor showing the left coronary system with annotations indicating 99% stenosis and a stent placement. The images are acquired at different oblique and cranial/caudal angles.

Annotations: 'Stenosis 99%' and 'Stent' visible
Multiple projection angles: RAO 1°/CRAN 45°, RAO 2°/CRAN 45°, RAO 2°/CAUD 45°, LAO 41°/CRAN 1°

Guidewires and catheter visible in vessel lumen
